title: London Branches
description: You are given three tables: companies, cities, and branches linking companies to cities. Find distinct names of companies that have at least one branch in 'London', ordered alphabetically.
inputFormat:
companies(id, name, industry)
cities(id, name, country)
branches(id, company_id, city_id, floor_count)
outputFormat: One company name per line, ordered alphabetically. No duplicates.
constraints: Use two JOINs and DISTINCT. Filter WHERE city name = 'London'. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT DISTINCT co.name FROM companies co JOIN branches b ON co.id = b.company_id JOIN cities c ON b.city_id = c.id WHERE c.name = 'London' ORDER BY co.name;
hint: Join companies → branches → cities, filter by city name = 'London', use DISTINCT.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE companies (id INTEGER, name TEXT, industry TEXT); CREATE TABLE cities (id INTEGER, name TEXT, country TEXT); CREATE TABLE branches (id INTEGER, company_id INTEGER, city_id INTEGER, floor_count INTEGER); INSERT INTO companies VALUES (1,'TechCorp','IT'),(2,'FinBank','Finance'),(3,'RetailCo','Retail'); INSERT INTO cities VALUES (1,'London','UK'),(2,'Paris','France'),(3,'Berlin','Germany'); INSERT INTO branches VALUES (1,1,1,5),(2,1,2,3),(3,2,3,2),(4,3,1,1);

CREATE TABLE companies (id INTEGER, name TEXT, industry TEXT); CREATE TABLE cities (id INTEGER, name TEXT, country TEXT); CREATE TABLE branches (id INTEGER, company_id INTEGER, city_id INTEGER, floor_count INTEGER); INSERT INTO companies VALUES (1,'Startup A','IT'),(2,'Global B','Finance'),(3,'Local C','Retail'); INSERT INTO cities VALUES (1,'London','UK'),(2,'Manchester','UK'),(3,'Edinburgh','UK'); INSERT INTO branches VALUES (1,1,2,2),(2,2,1,10),(3,2,3,5),(4,3,2,1);

CREATE TABLE companies (id INTEGER, name TEXT, industry TEXT); CREATE TABLE cities (id INTEGER, name TEXT, country TEXT); CREATE TABLE branches (id INTEGER, company_id INTEGER, city_id INTEGER, floor_count INTEGER); INSERT INTO companies VALUES (1,'Euro Corp','Trade'),(2,'Asia Inc','Tech'); INSERT INTO cities VALUES (1,'London','UK'),(2,'Paris','France'),(3,'Tokyo','Japan'); INSERT INTO branches VALUES (1,1,2,3),(2,2,3,5);

CREATE TABLE companies (id INTEGER, name TEXT, industry TEXT); CREATE TABLE cities (id INTEGER, name TEXT, country TEXT); CREATE TABLE branches (id INTEGER, company_id INTEGER, city_id INTEGER, floor_count INTEGER); INSERT INTO companies VALUES (1,'Alpha Co','IT'),(2,'Beta Inc','Finance'),(3,'Gamma Ltd','Retail'); INSERT INTO cities VALUES (1,'London','UK'),(2,'Birmingham','UK'); INSERT INTO branches VALUES (1,1,1,2),(2,2,1,4),(3,3,1,1);
testCases.expectedOutput:
Alpha Co
Beta Inc
Gamma Ltd

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE companies (id INTEGER, name TEXT, industry TEXT); CREATE TABLE cities (id INTEGER, name TEXT, country TEXT); CREATE TABLE branches (id INTEGER, company_id INTEGER, city_id INTEGER, floor_count INTEGER); INSERT INTO companies VALUES (1,'MegaCorp','Pharma'); INSERT INTO cities VALUES (1,'London','UK'),(2,'London','CA'); INSERT INTO branches VALUES (1,1,1,8);
